recommendations: add and run MCP smoke/integration tests in CI (test status is empty) covering SequenceTagger.predict
TextClassifier.predict
and ModelTrainer.train via the generated adapter
fix dependency detection in the analysis pipeline (it missed existing pyproject.toml/setup.py/requirements.txt) to avoid wrong packaging recommendations
reduce MCP endpoint surface by removing auto-exposed class-name aliases (e.g.
“sentence”
“sequencetagger”) and keep only stable method endpoints
add strict input/output schemas with Pydantic models plus versioned contracts for each endpoint
implement timeout/retry/error-mapping wrappers around heavy calls (transformer embedding/training) to improve MCP reliability
add authentication/authorization and rate limiting to MCP service before exposing model loading/training operations
pin and document compatibility matrix (Python/Torch/Transformers/FastMCP) and add a lockfile for reproducible plugin installs
create performance benchmarks (latency/memory) for key endpoints and add regression thresholds in CI
add structured logging and tracing (request id
model name
duration
failures) in mcp_service.py for observability
introduce caching/pooling for loaded models and embeddings to avoid repeated cold starts
add graceful resource guards in adapter (max batch size
max sequence length
GPU/CPU fallback) to prevent OOM on predict/train calls
improve README_MCP with concrete examples for each endpoint and failure modes
add security hardening for model/dataset loading paths (path validation
remote download policy)
add contract tests to ensure Sentence.to_dict/from_dict round-trip fidelity through MCP serialization
add a plugin lifecycle hook strategy using TrainerPlugin attach/detach to minimize intrusiveness during long training runs

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal status `success` and executed all planned nodes: download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize
Robust fallback behavior: when git clone failed, zip fallback succeeded (`processed_by: zip_fallback`, ~293 files discovered)
Import-based adapter strategy had high feasibility confidence (0.94), enabling automatic endpoint exposure
Generated MCP package structure and startup entrypoint were produced consistently
MCP plugin runtime check passed (`service_health: healthy`, transport: stdio)
failure_reasons: Primary repository acquisition failed in DownloadNode due to network/transport interruption (`curl 18`, early EOF, invalid index-pack)
Dependency detection pipeline produced false negatives (reported no pyproject/setup/requirements despite files existing)
Validation depth was low: original project tests not executed effectively (`passed: false`, no test files, execution_time 0)
Observability and performance metrics are incomplete (all zeros), limiting objective runtime quality assessment

node_performance.download_time: Download path was partially degraded: git clone failed, then recovered via zip fallback. This likely increased latency and reduced metadata fidelity.
node_performance.analysis_time: Analysis succeeded functionally, but quality was mixed due to incorrect dependency discovery and empty entry-point detection despite rich repo structure.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation completed quickly and produced expected files, but output quality signals are weak (`total_lines_of_code: 0`, `tool_endpoints: 0` inconsistent with listed endpoints).
node_performance.test_time: Test execution was minimal/insufficient; MCP health check passed, but no substantive integration or contract tests were recorded.

resource_usage.disk_usage: Likely low for generated artifacts, but reported generated size is zero, indicating instrumentation/data-quality issues.
